reasoning:
Baseline yield as of May 23, 2025, is 5.33% (after hitting 5.37% on May 21). Our forecast hinges on the tension between a Federal Reserve rate-cutting cycle and rising tail risks of inflation/fiscal deficits. 

In the short term (June 2025), yields are slightly more likely to decrease. While they experienced upward pressure in mid-May due to sticky inflation and fiscal concerns, a broad Reuters consensus of bond strategists (April 2025) expects 10-year yields to trend downward to ~4.21% by mid-year. Furthermore, the FRED linear 30-day projection anticipates lower rates, keeping the June probabilities below 50%. 

Over the longer term (August and November 2025), uncertainty grows substantially. Several institutions (such as ING and T. Rowe Price) warn that "tariff turmoil," expanding fiscal deficits, and growth scares could push the 10-year Treasury yield up toward 5%+, which would easily drag the ICE BofA Corporate Index yield above the 5.33% baseline. Consequently, to reflect this escalating volatility and the two-sided macro risk (Fed cuts vs. re-accelerating inflation), the probabilities for late 2025 are driven progressively closer to 50%.
